framework.name: HMG Minimum Cyber Security Standard
framework.scope: Mandatory minimum cyber security standard that all government departments and arm's length bodies must meet. Defines baseline requirements for identify, protect, detect, respond, and recover.
framework.structure_description: 10 requirements organised across 5 NIST CSF functions: identify, protect, detect, respond, and recover. Each requirement has sub-clauses.

controls.title: Identify and catalogue information assets
controls.description: Departments shall identify, catalogue, and manage information held in their systems and services, and the underlying technology on which they depend. Departments shall identify and record the user of each asset.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall identify, catalogue, and manage information held in their systems and services, and the underlying technology on which they depend. Departments shall identify and record the user of each asset.
controls.implementation_guidance: Maintain an asset register of information systems and data. Record ownership for each asset. Update the register when changes occur. Include both hardware and software assets.
controls.verification_guidance: Review asset register completeness. Verify ownership is assigned. Check register currency.

controls.title: Identify and manage risks to information assets
controls.title_nl: Identify and manage risks to information assets
controls.description: Departments shall identify, assess, and manage risks to their information and technology assets. Risk management shall be proportionate and integrated with existing risk management processes.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall identify, assess, and manage risks to their information and technology assets. Risk management shall be proportionate and integrated with existing risk management processes.
controls.implementation_guidance: Conduct risk assessments for information assets. Integrate cyber risks into the enterprise risk register. Review and update risks regularly. Define risk appetite.
controls.verification_guidance: Review risk assessments. Verify integration with enterprise risk register. Check review frequency.

controls.title: Control access to information and systems
controls.title_nl: Control access to information and systems
controls.description: Departments shall manage access to their information and technology services. Access rights shall be granted and removed in a timely manner using the principle of least privilege and need-to-know.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall manage access to their information and technology services. Access rights shall be granted and removed in a timely manner using the principle of least privilege and need-to-know.
controls.implementation_guidance: Apply least privilege. Implement joiner-mover-leaver processes. Use MFA for privileged and remote access. Review access rights regularly.
controls.verification_guidance: Audit access rights. Verify JML process timeliness. Check MFA enforcement.

controls.title: Protect data in transit
controls.description: Departments shall protect information in transit appropriately, including external and internal communications. As a minimum, all external internet-facing services must use TLS.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall protect information in transit appropriately, including external and internal communications. As a minimum, all external internet-facing services must use TLS.
controls.implementation_guidance: Use TLS on all internet-facing services. Implement email encryption where appropriate. Use DMARC with p=reject on all government domains.
controls.verification_guidance: Scan for TLS compliance. Verify DMARC policies. Check email encryption where required.

controls.title: Protect devices and software
controls.description: Departments shall maintain effective security management of their devices and software. Devices and software must be properly configured, patched, and maintained. Unsupported software shall be removed.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall maintain effective security management of their devices and software. Devices and software must be properly configured, patched, and maintained. Unsupported software shall be removed.
controls.implementation_guidance: Maintain a software inventory. Apply patches within defined timeframes. Remove unsupported software. Apply hardening baselines. Enable host-based firewalls.
controls.verification_guidance: Audit patch compliance. Verify no unsupported software. Check hardening baselines.

controls.title: Vulnerability management
controls.description: Departments shall implement a vulnerability disclosure and management process. Vulnerabilities shall be identified through scanning and disclosure, assessed for risk, and remediated within defined timeframes.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall implement a vulnerability disclosure and management process. Vulnerabilities shall be identified through scanning and disclosure, assessed for risk, and remediated within defined timeframes.
controls.implementation_guidance: Conduct regular vulnerability scanning. Triage vulnerabilities by risk. Patch critical vulnerabilities within 48 hours and high within 7 days. Implement a vulnerability disclosure policy.
controls.verification_guidance: Review scanning frequency. Check remediation timelines. Verify vulnerability disclosure policy.

controls.title: Monitor systems for security events
controls.description: Departments shall implement appropriate monitoring of networks and information systems to detect and respond to cyber security events. Logging and monitoring shall be sufficient to support incident response.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall implement appropriate monitoring of networks and information systems to detect and respond to cyber security events. Logging and monitoring shall be sufficient to support incident response.
controls.implementation_guidance: Deploy centralised log collection (SIEM). Monitor authentication events, network traffic, and system changes. Implement detection rules for known attack patterns. Retain logs for at least 12 months.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify monitoring coverage. Check SIEM deployment. Review detection rules. Confirm log retention.

controls.title: Have an incident response capability
controls.description: Departments shall have the capability to respond to and manage cyber security incidents. This includes having an incident response plan, trained staff, and the ability to report incidents to NCSC.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall have the capability to respond to and manage cyber security incidents. This includes having an incident response plan, trained staff, and the ability to report incidents to NCSC.
controls.implementation_guidance: Develop and maintain an incident response plan. Train incident response staff. Establish reporting channels to NCSC. Conduct regular exercises.
controls.verification_guidance: Review incident response plan. Verify staff training. Check NCSC reporting capability. Review exercise records.

controls.title: Report security incidents promptly
controls.description: Departments shall report significant cyber security incidents to NCSC promptly and to other relevant bodies (ICO, sector regulators) within mandated timeframes. Internal reporting channels shall be clear and well-communicated.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall report significant cyber security incidents to NCSC promptly and to other relevant bodies (ICO, sector regulators) within mandated timeframes. Internal reporting channels shall be clear and well-communicated.
controls.implementation_guidance: Establish incident reporting to NCSC. Know ICO notification requirements (72 hours for personal data breaches). Communicate internal reporting channels to all staff.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify NCSC reporting channel is established. Check regulatory notification procedures. Test internal reporting.

controls.title: Plan for recovery from cyber incidents
controls.title_nl: Plan for recovery from cyber incidents
controls.description: Departments shall have plans to recover from cyber security incidents. Plans shall include backup and restore capabilities, service continuity measures, and the ability to rebuild systems from a known-good state.
controls.description_nl: Departments shall have plans to recover from cyber security incidents. Plans shall include backup and restore capabilities, service continuity measures, and the ability to rebuild systems from a known-good state.
controls.implementation_guidance: Maintain tested backups. Define recovery time and recovery point objectives. Document recovery procedures. Test recovery at least annually.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify backup testing. Review recovery procedures. Check RTO/RPO documentation. Confirm annual recovery testing.
